title: Anatomic Disorders of the Chest and Airways

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องรู้ Anatomic Disorders of Chest and Airways?
ทารกที่ respiratory distress ไม่ตอบสนอง standard management → ต้อง think structural anomaly. (1) Newborn = obligate nasal breather 2–3 wk แรก → choanal atresia bilateral = emergency; (2) Laryngomalacia = most common stridor; (3) VCP หลัง PDA ligation 32% → ↑ BPD, feeding problems; (4) Tracheomalacia in BPD/preterm → CPAP + aortopexy; (5) EXIT procedure: controlled airway establishment ขณะ fetoplacental circulation intact; (6) CHARGE syndrome กับ choanal atresia: CHD7 gene mutation; (7) CDH: surgical after physiologic stability.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
วินิจฉัย + จัดการ nasopharyngeal obstruction: choanal atresia (CHARGE), pyriform aperture stenosis, Pierre Robin
อธิบาย laryngomalacia: pathophysiology, diagnosis, management (supraglottoplasty ถ้า severe)
อธิบาย vocal cord paralysis: ชนิด, etiologies (PDA ligation, birth trauma), management
รู้ tracheal disorders: tracheomalacia (aortopexy), congenital tracheal stenosis (slide tracheoplasty), vascular rings
อธิบาย CPAM / pulmonary sequestration / congenital lobar emphysema — presentations + management
รู้ EXIT procedure: indications (CHAOS), technique, outcomes
รู้ mediastinal masses by location: anterior (thymic) vs middle (bronchogenic cyst) vs posterior (neuroblastoma)
1. Nasopharyngeal Obstruction
1.1 Congenital Choanal Atresia

Choanal Atresia — CHARGE Association
Incidence: 1:5000–1:9000 births; female predominance. Bilateral: emergency at birth (newborn obligate nasal breather). Unilateral: presents later with unilateral discharge + feeding difficulties. Composition: mixed bony + membranous (majority). > 50% associated with CHARGE syndrome: CHD7 gene (chromosome 8) — Coloboma, Heart defects, Atresia choanae, Retardation, Genital abnormalities, Ear anomalies. Dx: 5-6Fr catheter cannot pass OR nasofiberscopy + CT nasopharynx. Management: oropharyngeal airway ± ETI; surgical repair (transnasal endoscopic — preferred; transpalatal for thick bony lesions).
1.2 Nasal Pyriform Aperture Stenosis

Rare; excessive bone formation ใน medial nasal processes of maxillary bone. Association: solitary median maxillary incisor + holoprosencephaly + pituitary hypoplasia. Dx: CT nasopharynx; brain MRI ถ้า midline defects suspected. Rx: nasal decongestants ถ้า mild; sublabial surgery ถ้า aperture < 5.7 mm
1.3 Pierre Robin Syndrome (Robin Sequence)
Triad: cleft palate + micrognathia + glossoptosis → pharyngeal obstruction. Incidence: 1:8500–1:30,000. Linked to SOX9 and KCNJ2 mutations

Mechanism: hypoplastic mandible → tongue displaced posteriorly → worst supine + during active sleep + feeds. Chronic obstruction → CO2 retention + failure to thrive + pulmonary hypertension + RV failure

Management graduated: (1) prone positioning (mild); (2) nasopharyngeal airway 3.5-mm ETT through nose into hypopharynx (moderate — for weeks/months); (3) tongue-lip adhesion (glossopexy); (4) mandibular distraction; (5) tracheostomy ถ้า all fail. Usually resolves 3–12 months as mandible grows + tongue control improves
2. Laryngeal Anomalies
2.1 Laryngomalacia
Most common cause of stridor in infants — prolapse of supraglottic structures (arytenoids, aryepiglottic folds, epiglottis) into airway during inspiration
Presentation: loud, high-pitched inspiratory stridor, onset first month; worse supine + agitated; better prone + neck extended; usually no cyanosis or significant feeding difficulty in mild cases
Worst at 4–8 months; most resolve conservatively ~12–24 months. Synchronous anomalies in 30%: VCP, tracheomalacia, subglottic stenosis → evaluate full airway
Dx: flexible fiberoptic laryngoscopy (gold standard) + laryngeal US (adjunct). CT scan ถ้า needed
Management: conservative (most), acid suppression (GERD component). Severe (20%): supraglottoplasty → 71% complete resolution, 94% symptom improvement; tracheostomy สำหรับ failures
2.2 Vocal Cord Paralysis (VCP)

Unilateral (left > right): often presents as aspiration (weak laryngeal closure → cough/choke during feeds). Iatrogenic causes: excessive neck stretch delivery, PDA ligation (RLN injury — 32% of VLBW after ligation); ↑ BPD, feeding problems, GTube placement. Right VCP: cervical ECMO cannulation complication

Bilateral VCP: serious — high-pitched inspiratory stridor, normal cry. Causes: CNS (HIE, IVH, Arnold-Chiari, hydrocephalus). Associated: pharyngeal incoordination, apnea, aspiration. 50–60% require tracheostomy. Poor prognosis secondary to underlying CNS pathology

Dx: flexible fiberoptic laryngoscopy (awake preferred). Management: speech/swallow therapy; unilateral may improve weeks–months. Medialization (injection laryngoplasty/thyroplasty) ≥ 6 months age for severe aspiration
2.3 Subglottic Stenosis
Congenital: incomplete cricoid canalization. Dx: subglottic diameter ≤ 4 mm (term) or ≤ 3 mm (preterm). Rx: balloon dilation ± anterior cricoid split (avoids tracheostomy in most)
Acquired (more common in NICU): prolonged intubation + injury to subglottis. Prevention: correct ETT size, minimize movement
Subglottic hemangioma: asymmetric subglottic narrowing; beta blockers (propranolol) = first-line → regression, prevents tracheostomy; previously steroids ± KTP laser
2.4 Laryngotracheoesophageal Cleft

1:10,000–1:20,000; failed fusion posterior cricoid lamina → communication between airway + esophagus. Presents: respiratory distress + stridor + aspiration of saliva/feeds; aspiration pneumonia on CXR

Classification: Type I–IV by severity. Associated: VACTERL, TE fistula, Opitz-Frias. Management: secure airway; endoscopic injection augmentation (mild); anterior translaryngotracheal reconstruction (severe). Mortality up to 25%
3. Tracheal Disorders
3.1 Tracheomalacia

Tracheomalacia in Preterm NICU
Primary tracheomalacia: delayed cartilage development → dynamic tracheal collapse during expiration. Secondary (NICU infants): associated with BPD, prolonged intubation, TEF, extrinsic compression. Presentation: expiratory stridor, cyanotic spells, recurrent LRTI, persistent wet cough. Dx: bronchoscopy = gold standard (anterior+posterior tracheal wall approximation during expiration). CT scan 62% sensitive only. Management: many improve by 1–2 yr (cartilage strengthens). Severe: CPAP/PEEP (prevents collapse) + anterior aortopexy (trachea pulled forward). Posterior tracheopexy + aortopexy for posterior membranous intrusion.
3.2 Congenital Tracheal Stenosis
1:64,500; complete cartilage rings → narrow trachea. ↑ with vascular ring anomalies, CHD, TE fistula (H-type), pulmonary agenesis. Inspiratory stridor + expiratory wheeze + cyanotic episodes
Dx: flexible bronchoscopy (gold standard); rigid bronchoscopy in OR; ultrafast CT (3D reconstruction for surgical planning). Tracheostomy contraindicated — interferes with diagnosis
Slide tracheoplasty: standard of care for long-segment stenosis. Balloon dilation for short segment. Survival > 80% with CPB + modern technique (↑ mortality ถ้า concurrent distal bronchomalacia)
3.3 Vascular Rings

Tracheal compression by: (1) double aortic arch (most complete); (2) right aortic arch + ligamentum arteriosus; (3) left innominate artery; (4) pulmonary artery sling (left PA from right PA — passes between trachea + esophagus)
Presentation: inspiratory stridor + expiratory wheeze (appear later in neonatal period); head/neck hyperextended; esophageal compression → regurgitation
Dx: barium swallow (esophageal indentation) + CT/MRI (define anatomy). Bronchoscopy: pulsatile mass compressing trachea near carina. Prenatal US diagnosis → ↓ delay in repair
Rx: surgical division (VATS approach increasingly used). Post-repair: tracheal deformity may persist weeks → monitor. Isolated vascular ring repair: minimal mortality, limited complications
4. Chest Anomalies
4.1 Congenital Pulmonary Airway Malformation (CPAM) — Previously CCAM

Hamartomatous lesions arising from bronchial overgrowth; classified Type 0–4 (Stocker). Type 2 (small cysts < 2 cm) most common. May contain systemic arterial supply (hybrid lesion with sequestration)

Presentation: prenatal US (most) → management guided by size, mediastinal shift, hydrops. Asymptomatic at birth → often resect electively. Symptomatic: respiratory distress, air trapping, ↑ cyst size postnatally

CXR: multicystic mass ± mediastinal shift. CT chest at age 1–3 months for full characterization. Rx: elective thoracoscopic or open lobectomy → ↓ infection + ↓ malignant transformation (very rare, < 1%)
4.2 Pulmonary Sequestration
Non-functioning lung tissue with anomalous systemic arterial supply (from descending aorta usually). Intralobar (no separate pleura, 75%) vs extralobar (separate pleura, associated anomalies, 25%)
Intralobar: presents in older children with recurrent pneumonia. Extralobar: usually left lower lobe; can be subdiaphragmatic; associated with CDH, CHD
Dx: US Doppler (systemic artery from aorta) + CT/MRI. Rx: resection (VATS or open); ensure arterial supply identified before surgery — ligation of systemic artery essential
4.3 Congenital Lobar Emphysema (CLE)

Progressive hyperinflation of one lobe (left upper > right middle > right upper). Causes: cartilage deficiency in lobar bronchus → air trapping; extrinsic bronchial compression (bronchogenic cyst, vascular); mucus plug

Presentation: tachypnea + respiratory distress ± mediastinal shift. CXR: hyperinflated lobe + compression atelectasis of adjacent lobes + mediastinal shift. May simulate tension pneumothorax — do NOT insert chest drain until imaging excludes CLE
Rx: lobectomy (definitive); bronchoscopy to exclude mucus plug/intrinsic lesion first. Urgent ถ้า severe respiratory distress
4.4 Bronchogenic Cysts
Foregut cysts; located middle mediastinum (most) near carina. Lined with columnar epithelium, contain smooth muscle + cartilage. May compress airway → atelectasis or air trapping
CXR: posterior mass near carina. CT: sharply marginated cystic water-density mass. Barium swallow: esophageal indentation at carina level
Rx: surgical excision (open or VATS) — prevents adult-onset symptoms + rare malignant degeneration
5. EXIT Procedure and CHAOS

EXIT (Ex Utero Intrapartum Treatment) Procedure
CHAOS (Congenital High Airway Obstruction Syndrome): spectrum of anomalies causing complete/near-complete airway obstruction: laryngeal web, laryngeal atresia, tracheal atresia/stenosis, subglottic cyst. Prenatal US: enlarged hyperechoic lungs + flattened/inverted diaphragm + polyhydramnios ± hydrops fetalis. EXIT procedure: scheduled cesarean section with preservation of fetoplacental circulation while fetal airway is established (laryngoscopy + bronchoscopy + intubation ± tracheostomy under direct vision). Then umbilical cord ligated after safe airway confirmed. High chance of survival with EXIT if no other lethal malformations. Followed by definitive airway reconstruction (elective).
6. Mediastinal Masses

rows: Thymic hyperplasia, thymoma, lymphoma, teratoma, thymic cyst
Normal thymus ≠ tracheal deviation; involutes with steroids; thymic carcinoma 21% 5-yr survival
Bronchogenic cyst, esophageal duplication cyst, neurenteric cyst, lymphadenopathy
Posterior to carina; barium swallow shows esophageal indentation
Neuroblastoma #1 solid mediastinal tumor in infants; Horner syndrome; N-myc amplification = poor prognosis

blocks.text: Congenital mediastinal teratoma: rare; mature teratomas not malignant; resect via sternotomy or VATS. Immature: resect ± chemo. Fetal hydrops → EXIT + resection near term

Neuroblastoma: ~20% of neuroblastomas are thoracic; posterior mediastinum along sympathetic chain. Urinary VMA/HVA; MIBG scintigraphy for staging. INRG staging L1/L2/M/MS; risk stratification → surgery ± chemo ± radio ± immunotherapy
7. NICU Practical — Airway & Chest Problems

Bilateral choanal atresia: cyanosis relieved by opening mouth/crying → insert oral McGovern nipple airway immediately; ETI ถ้าจำเป็น; NG tube for feeds; refer ENT for surgical repair within days; work up CHARGE (CHD7, echo, ophthalmology, audiology)

Stridor in neonate: inspiratory = supraglottic (laryngomalacia) or subglottic stenosis; expiratory = tracheomalacia; biphasic = tracheal/subglottic. Flexible laryngoscopy + bronchoscopy needed for definitive Dx. Check synchronous anomalies

After PDA ligation: 32% VCP (VLBW). Screen: flexible laryngoscopy before discharge ถ้า feeding difficulties post-ligation. Left VCP: lay on affected side (cord falls away from midline → ↓ obstruction); speech/swallow therapy; modified feeds
Tracheomalacia + BPD: CPAP 4–6 cmH2O splints trachea → ↓ expiratory collapse; ↑ PEEP on MV. Aortopexy ถ้า severe (life-threatening episodes)
CLE vs tension pneumothorax: hyperinflated lobe + mediastinal shift. Do NOT insert chest drain blind — CXR ± CT to differentiate. CLE → urgent lobectomy if compromised

CPAM prenatal Dx: if hydrops → consider fetal intervention (resection or pleuroamniotic shunt). If no hydrops at birth: CT chest at 1–3 months → elective thoracoscopic resection at 3–6 months recommended by most centers to prevent infection + rare malignancy
8. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Cyanosis Relieved by Crying = Bilateral Choanal Atresia
Newborn cyanotic at rest แต่ better ขณะร้องไห้ = bilateral choanal atresia จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่. ทดสอบ: 5-6Fr catheter cannot pass through nostril. Insert oral airway ทันที; NG tube for feeds; surgical repair ภายใน days. Work up CHARGE (CHD7 gene + echo + eyes + ears).

PEARL 2 — PDA Ligation → 32% VCP — Screen ก่อน Discharge
RLN injury ระหว่าง PDA ligation dissection → left VCP ใน 32% ของ VLBW. ผล: ↑ BPD risk, feeding difficulties, ↑ GTube placement. Flexible laryngoscopy ก่อน discharge ทุก VLBW ที่ผ่าน PDA ligation. Unilateral VCP: lay on affected side + speech/swallow therapy → ส่วนมาก improve months.

PEARL 3 — Laryngomalacia Synchronous Lesions 30%
Laryngomalacia ≠ isolated. 30% มี synchronous airway anomaly: VCP, tracheomalacia, subglottic stenosis. ตรวจ full airway (nasopharynx → trachea → carina) ด้วย flexible bronchoscopy ก่อน diagnose isolated laryngomalacia. Severe laryngomalacia (20%): supraglottoplasty → ≥ 71% complete resolution; tracheostomy สำหรับ failures.

PEARL 4 — CLE ≠ Tension Pneumothorax — ห้าม Chest Drain Blind
CLE + mediastinal shift อาจดู identical กับ tension pneumothorax บน CXR. ถ้า insert chest drain ใน CLE → lung collapse + respiratory failure worse. ทำ CXR ± CT ก่อน intervention เสมอ. CLE: hyperinflated lobe + compression of adjacent lobes (ไม่มี pleural air line). Urgent lobectomy ถ้า compromised.

PEARL 5 — EXIT ช่วย CHAOS — Controlled Airway ขณะ Still on Placenta
CHAOS (laryngeal/tracheal atresia/stenosis/web): fetus will have complete airway obstruction at birth. EXIT procedure: scheduled C-section + maintain fetoplacental circulation while airway established (laryngoscopy + bronchoscopy + intubation ± tracheostomy under direct vision; cord cut AFTER airway secured). High survival ถ้าไม่มี other lethal anomalies. Prenatal US/MRI diagnosis → plan EXIT delivery at tertiary center.
9. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Bhatt JM et al. Cochrane 2019: CHD7 mutation in 60% of CHARGE syndrome patients; CHD7 testing recommended for all bilateral choanal atresia.
Wootten CT et al. Pediatrics 2006: Supraglottoplasty outcomes: 71% complete resolution, 94% symptom improvement at follow-up.
Clement WA et al. Int J Pediatr Otorhinolaryngol 2016: Systematic review VCP after PDA ligation: 32% incidence in VLBW on laryngoscopy; ↑ BPD, feeding problems.
Benjamin JR et al. J Perinatol 2010: Left VCP after PDA surgical ligation: associated with ↑ risk of BPD and GTube dependence.
Backer CL et al. Ann Thorac Surg 2012: Slide tracheoplasty for long-segment tracheal stenosis: survival > 80%; concurrent distal bronchomalacia predicts worse outcome.

Saadai P et al. J Pediatr Surg. 2012;47:1095: Long-term outcomes after fetal therapy for CHAOS: EXIT procedure + airway reconstruction — high survival rates in selected cases without other lethal anomalies.
Hirose S et al. J Pediatr Surg. 2004;39:375: EXIT procedure technique review — airway established while fetoplacental circulation intact; low hypoxemia complications.
Durell J et al. Arch Dis Child 2020: CPAM management: elective resection recommended to prevent infection and rare malignant transformation (pleuropulmonary blastoma risk in type 1).
Parelkar SV et al. J Pediatr Surg 2012: Aortopexy for tracheomalacia: good outcomes; thoracoscopic approach feasible; posterior tracheopexy for posterior membranous tracheal collapse.
Wells LM et al. Semin Pediatr Surg 2017 (vascular rings): Prenatal diagnosis of vascular ring → early repair prevented secondary tracheobronchomalacia development.
10. Self-Check Questions
Q: ทารกคลอดมี cyanosis ขณะพัก แต่ดีขึ้นเมื่อร้องไห้ — Dx และ management ฉุกเฉิน?

ตอบ: Bilateral choanal atresia — newborn obligate nasal breather; ร้องไห้ opens mouth → O2 via oral route. การจัดการทันที: (1) insert oral airway (McGovern nipple or modified ETT/OPA); (2) NG tube สำหรับ feeds; (3) suction nasopharynx; (4) ETI ถ้า oral airway ไม่เพียงพอ; (5) work up CHARGE: CHD7 gene, cardiac echo, ophthalmology (coloboma), audiology (ear anomalies), growth assessment; (6) ENT consult — surgical repair transnasal endoscopic within days of stabilization.
Q: Pierre Robin syndrome: pathophysiology ของ airway obstruction และ management approach?

ตอบ: Micrognathia → posterior displacement of tongue (glossoptosis) → pharyngeal obstruction. Worst supine/active sleep (pharyngeal muscle tone absent). Chronic obstruction → CO2 retention → failure to thrive → cor pulmonale. Management graduated: (1) prone positioning; (2) nasopharyngeal airway (3.5 mm ETT through nose into hypopharynx) — prevents negative suction; left for weeks/months; (3) glossopexy; (4) mandibular distraction; (5) tracheostomy. Condition usually resolves 3–12 months as mandible grows.
Q: Tracheomalacia ใน NICU infant — diagnosis + management?

ตอบ: Bronchoscopy = gold standard: dynamic approximation of anterior + posterior tracheal walls during expiration. CT sensitivity only 62%. Risk factors in NICU: BPD, prolonged intubation, high MAP, prematurity. Management: (1) CPAP/PEEP (prevents dynamic collapse); many improve by 1–2 yr as cartilage strengthens. Severe: (2) anterior aortopexy (fixation aorta to sternum → pulls trachea forward; via left anterior thoracotomy or VATS); (3) posterior tracheopexy ถ้า posterior membranous collapse. Stents: limited use due to migration/erosion.
Q: CPAM: prenatal management ถ้าพบ fetal hydrops? postnatal elective management?

ตอบ: Fetal hydrops (venous return obstruction from large CPAM): fetal intervention: thoracoamniotic shunt หรือ fetal resection ถ้า large cystic lesion. Postnatal no hydrops: CT chest 1–3 months for characterization; elective thoracoscopic lobectomy 3–6 months recommended to: (1) prevent recurrent infection; (2) prevent rare malignant transformation (pleuropulmonary blastoma type 1 CPAM); (3) allow compensatory lung growth. Some centers observe asymptomatic CPAM + resect only if symptomatic.
Q: EXIT procedure: สำหรับ CHAOS ทำอะไรบ้าง และ timing?

ตอบ: EXIT (Ex Utero Intrapartum Treatment): planned cesarean section; fetus partially delivered; fetoplacental circulation maintained (placenta still perfusing). ทำ direct laryngoscopy + bronchoscopy + intubation (± tracheostomy) while fetus on placenta. Cord ligated AFTER airway secured. Indication: CHAOS (laryngeal/tracheal atresia, web, cyst). High survival ถ้าไม่มี other lethal anomalies. Prenatal MRI/US ช่วย plan procedure. Tertiary center ที่มี pediatric surgery + ENT + neonatology ready. Postnatal: definitive airway reconstruction เมื่อ stable.
11. Day 45 Preview

Avery Chapter 45 — Developmental Biology of the Heart
พรุ่งนี้เรียน Developmental Biology of the Heart — เรียนตั้งแต่ cardiac crescent → heart tube → looping → septation → complete 4-chamber heart. เนื้อหาสำคัญ: primary/secondary heart field, neural crest contribution, endocardial cushions, conotruncal defects, DiGeorge syndrome (22q11/TBX1), heterotaxy, PFO/ASD, ventricular septum, aortic arch development. ความสำคัญ clinical: เข้าใจ embryology → เข้าใจ CHD จาก root cause. เชื่อมกับ Ch44: CHARGE syndrome ทำให้เราเห็น CHD7 → cardiac anomalies; CDH → cardiac anomalies; DiGeorge → conotruncal + arch defects.
